What's going on everyone? Okay, so this is a very quick video, but I want to explain this in a little bit more detail. I already talked about the different plays in the overview, but ultimately you're going to have a front-end hook and a back-end hook. And what I mean by that is that the front-end hook is whatever we marketed it externally, and the back-end hook is what we're going to market to them internally to get them to stay longer on the back-end. So we're using that as bait to get them to stay and continue on their membership. So basically, so say someone signs up for a 42-day detox, right? And they're at the halfway point. What we want to do there is give them on the back-end of the basically the halfway point, it's like, awesome, after you've finished the end, whichever one you're using on the back-end, you don't need to do any of that stuff from the weight loss, money-back component at all. They should already be excited and you just say, like, hey, this is the new program, this is the program you do after this. This is what's going to recalibrate you. And it also provides an opportunity for you to explain that this is a system, right? This is really just step one, and you really can't just do step one. You need step two as well, because we need to recalibrate you. We need to get you, we need to reset your metabolism. We need to get you, basically, we don't want you to gain the weight back. And so we need to create the optimal environment so you can keep the weight off, and that's what step two is about. Okay? So hopefully that makes sense. I'll get more into the actual selling pieces of that, but I just want you to understand the overarching structure of which offer you're putting on the front-end or the back-end. It does not matter which order you're doing it in. The point is one of them is going to be what you're marketing, and the other one is going to be what you're going to be marketing to them once they are customers. All right? And this list is not exhausted. We'll probably add more big plays to this, but this is just a starter for you to get going.
